slides.content: **Purpose** The purpose of this lab is to provide participants with knowledge to use and monitor SLAM-R virtualized ICS offerings. **Task** 1\. Complete the lab below. 2\. Work through the Steps below. 3\. Participate in classroom discussion. 4\. Review with the class. 5\. Load the following scenario: :::scenario ```json { "uuid": "e3bc96c8-392f-4433-8566-969e6a879c42", "name": "test-autograders", "promptClass": false, "moveOnCriteria": "completed", "rc5id": "20251120135511-851f4365-3cd9-42f4-8995-80a214dbdba1" } ``` ::: **Steps** ***Access the HMI*** 1\. Go to the HMI. The username is “*cents*” and the password is “*P@55w0rd!*”. Observe that the Smart building Control System is displayed, as shown in Figure 1. Figure 1. 2\. Observe the Keypad Control display.  From here you can access or lock specific rooms within the building by selecting the light for the desired room, as shown in Figure 2. Figure 2. 3\. Select various buttons and observe its reactions within the Keypad Controller. 4\. Observe Elevator Control. There are three elevators in our Smart Building they can be stopped by pressing the red Stop Button to the right of each meter. Notice Elevator West has no indication on the meter so it is out of service, as shown in Figure 3. Figure 3. 5\. Press "Reset All" and observe that Elevator West changes.  This is true for all devices in this section. 6\. Observe HVAC Control. There are three floors to our Smart Building and each floors master HVAC is called out here. Press any of the buttons here to see how the controls are managed to make adjustments, as shown in Figure 4. Figure 4. 7\. The Last section of the HMI is "Alarm Status". Press any of the buttons here to see how these controls are managed, as shown in Figure 5. Figure 5. ***Access the PLC and controls*** 8\. Now go to the PLC. Figure 6. 9\. Observe this PLC has no GUI and is a barebones server. Log into the PLC using the credentials below: *- root* *- P@55w0rd!* 10\. Areas to observe: Host Name and Server IP address.  These files are located in the directories shown below: *cat /etc/hostname*  *cat /etc/network/interfaces* Figure 7. ***Note:*** These files are set when the PLC is set after the image is deployed. Host name and IP have a one up for any deployment with multiple nodes. *i.e., Node-Alpha (ip 10.120.19.101/24)* \*\* \*\* *Node-Bravo (ip 10.120.19.102/24)* \*\* \*\* *etc...* ***Monitoring PLC and HMI communications*** This can be done with several 3rd party tools such as Wireshark. In this lab, we will use the resident console with tcpdump with a pipe "|" grep command. 12\. Type the following inside command in your open console window: *tcpdump -i eth0 | grep 502* ***Note:*** All nodes use eth0 and port 502 for Modbus communications. 13\. Observe the output for normal operations you should only see communications from HMI to PLC. If there are communications captured using an IP other than those designated this will require further investigation for a possible Compromise. This is the completion of the Lab.
